# Checklist — Writing a Well-Formed Individual Requirement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018)

> Reference checklist derived from **ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 §5.2.4, §5.2.5, §5.2.7** — applied **per requirement** (the document-/set-level checks are in [checklist-iso-conformance.md](checklist-iso-conformance.md)).
> **Ordered by severity of a *missing* check** (Critical → High → Medium → Low) using the same severity scale.

## Checklist

| ID | Check name | ISO reference | Severity | What must be verified | Concrete example (✅ success / ❌ failure) |
|----|------------|---------------|----------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| IR-001 | Subject + verb present; the requirement names what (the system / software / element) and what shall be done or constrained | §5.2.4 | Critical | The statement names the **subject** (the system, the software, the API, …), uses a **mandatory verb** (`shall`), and states what is done or the constraint. | ✅ "The login API shall respond within 300 ms at the 95th percentile under 1 000 concurrent users." ❌ "Fast logins." (no subject, no verb, no quantity). |
| IR-002 | Uses **shall** for mandatory binding; not "must", not "shall not", not "shall be able to" | §5.2.4 | Critical | "shall" is used for the mandatory binding provision. "must" is avoided; negative requirements ("shall not") are reformulated positively; "shall be able to" is avoided. | ✅ "The system shall reject withdrawals when balance is below the requested amount." ❌ "The system must not allow overdrafts." |
| IR-003 | Singular — one decision per requirement | §5.2.5 (Singular) | Critical | The requirement states **one** capability / characteristic / constraint / quality factor. Compound statements with "and / or" are decomposed into separate requirements. | ✅ Two requirements: "FR-12a The system shall send a password-reset email." / "FR-12b The system shall send a password-reset SMS." ❌ "The system shall send a password-reset email and SMS, except for admins who use the helpdesk." |
| IR-004 | Verifiable — realisation can be demonstrated, ideally measurable | §5.2.4, §5.2.5 (Verifiable) | Critical | The requirement is structured and worded so its realisation can be proven at the level it exists. **Measurable when possible.** | ✅ "Search results page shall render in ≤ 800 ms at p95 for catalogs ≤ 1M items." ❌ "Search shall be responsive." |
| IR-005 | Unambiguous — interpretable in only one way | §5.2.5 (Unambiguous) | Critical | A reasonable reader produces the same interpretation. Avoid vague pronouns ("it", "this"), pre-supposed knowledge, double meanings. | ✅ "When the customer submits a refund request within 14 calendar days of the purchase date, the system shall …" ❌ "It shall be supported when appropriate." |
| IR-006 | Complete — describes what is needed without needing other information | §5.2.5 (Complete) | Critical | The requirement is self-contained; no implicit context needed to understand it. References are explicit (with date / version / clause). | ✅ "On HTTP 5xx, the system shall retry per the exponential-backoff policy defined in REQ-NFR-018." ❌ "On error, retry as appropriate." |
| IR-007 | No vague terms or unbounded language | §5.2.7 | Critical | The statement is free of: superlatives (best, most), subjective language (user friendly, easy to use, cost effective), vague pronouns (it, this, that), ambiguous adverbs (almost always, significant, minimal), ambiguous logical operators (or, and/or), open-ended terms (provide support, but not limited to, as a minimum), comparative phrases (better than, higher quality), loopholes (if possible, as appropriate, as applicable), totality terms (all, always, never, every), incomplete references. | ✅ "The system shall support the 10 user roles listed in Table 4." ❌ "The system shall support all roles, as appropriate, and shall always be user-friendly." |
| IR-008 | States **what**, not **how** (no design solution embedded) | §5.2.7 | High | The requirement describes the *need* at the appropriate level — not a specific solution (technology, algorithm, framework). Solution choices belong in design. | ✅ "The system shall encrypt PII at rest." ❌ "The system shall use AES-256-GCM provided by library X v1.2 in module Y." (over-specifies the design.) |
| IR-009 | Necessary — removing it would leave a real deficiency | §5.2.5 (Necessary) | High | The requirement defines an essential capability / characteristic / constraint / quality. If removed, the entity need is not fulfilled by any other requirement. Not made obsolete by passage of time. | ✅ The requirement traces back to a stakeholder need that nothing else covers. ❌ Two near-duplicate requirements; removing one changes nothing. |
| IR-010 | Appropriate — abstraction level matches the entity | §5.2.5 (Appropriate) | High | The requirement's intent and amount of detail fit the level (business / stakeholder / system / system element / software). No unnecessary constraints on architecture or design; implementation independence preserved. | ✅ Business requirement: "The system shall enable monthly reconciliation between booked transactions and bank statements." ❌ Same intent stated at business level as "The PostgreSQL transactions table shall have a `bank_ref` index." |
| IR-011 | Feasible — realisable within constraints with acceptable risk | §5.2.5 (Feasible) | High | The requirement can be implemented within cost / schedule / technical constraints. Conflicts with other requirements are surfaced. | ✅ "p95 ≤ 800 ms at 1 k RPS, target met by k=3 cache replicas in the regional plan." ❌ "Sub-1ms global p99 latency in a single region on commodity hardware." |
| IR-012 | Correct — accurate representation of the source need | §5.2.5 (Correct) | High | The requirement reflects the actual stakeholder need from which it was transformed; reviewed and confirmed by the source. | ✅ Linked back to interview transcript SME-014 and confirmed in writing. ❌ Engineer-invented requirement nobody asked for. |
| IR-013 | Conditions explicit and measurable when present | §5.2.4 (Conditions) | High | Qualifying conditions ("under load", "during peak", "after 90 days") are measurable and bounded. | ✅ "Under sustained load of 1 000 concurrent users for ≥ 10 min, the system shall …" ❌ "Sometimes under heavy load, the system shall …" |
| IR-014 | Constraints separated from functional behaviour | §5.2.4 (Constraints) | High | Constraints are distinguished from behaviour — either listed with the requirement, in a relationship, or as stand-alone requirements. | ✅ "[CONSTR] The system shall be deployed on Kubernetes ≥ 1.28." ❌ "The system shall reject invalid input and shall be deployed on Kubernetes." |
| IR-015 | Active voice | §5.2.4 | Medium | The statement uses the active voice ("The system shall …"); passive constructions ("It is required that …") are reformulated. | ✅ "The system shall log every administrative action." ❌ "Logging of administrative actions is required." |
| IR-016 | Conforming — follows the project's approved template / style for requirements | §5.2.5 (Conforming) | Medium | The requirement adheres to the approved template, syntax, ID scheme, and attribute structure used by the project. | ✅ Each requirement uses the team template: ID, statement (`shall`), conditions, rationale, source, type, priority, owner, risk. ❌ Free-form prose with no structure. |
| IR-017 | Identification: unique ID, never changed, never reused | §5.2.8.2 (Identification) | Medium | Each requirement carries a unique identifier. The identifier does not change when the requirement is reworded, and is not reused after deletion. | ✅ `REQ-FR-0421` immutable across versions. ❌ Renumbered every release when a row is deleted. |
| IR-018 | Owner attribute populated | §5.2.8.2 (Owner) | Medium | Each requirement names an owner (person or organisational element) who maintains it, approves changes, reports status. | ✅ Owner = "Risk Office — Maria Bianchi". ❌ Owner blank; nobody can approve a change. |
| IR-019 | Stakeholder Priority attribute populated | §5.2.8.2 (Stakeholder Priority) | Medium | Each requirement has a priority value (e.g., 1..5 or High / Medium / Low) reached via consensus with the relevant stakeholders. | ✅ "Priority: High (agreed by sponsor + ops in steering committee 2026-04-12)." ❌ All requirements "High"; team picks whatever they like first. |
| IR-020 | Rationale recorded | §5.2.8.2 (Rationale) | Medium | The reason the requirement is needed is captured, with pointer to analysis / trade study / modelling / regulator clause. | ✅ "Rationale: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure)." ❌ Rationale blank; team can't decide whether to drop the requirement when scope slips. |
| IR-021 | Type attribute populated | §5.2.8.2 / §5.2.8.3 (Type) | Medium | Requirement is tagged with one of the standard types: Functional/Performance, Interface, Process, Quality (Non-Functional / "-ility"), Usability/Quality-in-Use, Human Factors. | ✅ Tag `[NFR/Performance]`. ❌ Untagged; impossible to filter for review by domain experts. |
| IR-022 | Risk attribute considered (esp. for at-risk requirements) | §5.2.8.2 (Risk) | Medium | Requirements at risk of failing verification or validation carry a risk value (potentially inherited from a parent requirement). | ✅ "Risk: High — relies on new vendor library v0.x; risk inherited from REQ-FR-099." ❌ All risk fields blank; first defect at integration is a surprise. |
| IR-023 | Version Number tracked for the requirement | §5.2.8.2 (Version Number) | Low | The requirement's version number reflects how often it has changed; high volatility is a signal to investigate. | ✅ Tool tracks version per requirement; weekly report flags volatile ones. ❌ Last-changed date but no version; volatility invisible. |
| IR-024 | Difficulty attribute populated | §5.2.8.2 (Difficulty) | Low | Easy / Nominal / Difficult marker, useful for cost modelling and capacity planning. | ✅ Difficulty = Difficult → flagged for spike. ❌ Field blank; estimation skipped. |
| IR-025 | Assumptions documented in the requirement's rationale or accompanying document | §5.2.7 | Low | Any assumption underlying the requirement is recorded and validated. | ✅ "Assumes upstream service publishes events in JSON Schema v2 — confirmed with team X 2026-04-04." ❌ Implicit assumption; first breaking change blows up integration. |
